claim.content: The DMC effective masses show opposite trends with increasing $r_s$ (decreasing density) for the two spin polarizations studied: for the paramagnetic 2D HEG $m^{*}$ increases with increasing interaction strength over the range examined (from approximately $0.95$ at $r_s=1$ to approximately $1.34$ at $r_s=10$), whereas for the fully spin-polarized (ferromagnetic) 2D HEG $m^{*}$ decreases with increasing $r_s$ (from approximately $0.86$ at $r_s=1$ to approximately $0.61$ at $r_s=10$). These calculated trends are consistent with experimental measurements that report an increase of the paramagnetic effective mass as density is lowered (Tan et al., Phys. Rev. Lett. 94, 016405 (2005)) and a decrease of the effective mass in ferromagnetic or partially spin-polarized samples as density is lowered (Padmanabhan et al., Phys. Rev. Lett. 101, 026402 (2008) and related work).
